title: Carbon Isotope Discrimination Gas Analysis
author: Stephen Driever
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qwudxew
materials: Do this for both Sample and Ref side
Write values down in lab book

steps.title: Open inlet valve to vacuum (RL)
steps.instruction: Open inlet valve to vacuum (RL)

steps.title: Switch between Ref and Sample (2x F1) Reference gas
steps.instruction: Switch between Ref and Sample (2x F1) Reference gas

steps.title: Manually open valve and fill the...
steps.instruction: Manually open valve and fill the reference loop with gas Make sure the second valve (top of image) is closed so gas only fills the reference loop. Open the first valve (depicted) for ~5s to fill collection loop.

steps.title: Close the valve to the reference gas
steps.instruction: Close the valve to the reference gas

steps.title: Open the second valve, to let...
steps.instruction: Open the second valve, to let the reference gas from the connector piece into the inlet Make sure the first valve is closed, then open up the second valve (depicted) to let reference gas out of collection loop into the mass spec. You should see a change in the beam intensity as gas is detected. Wait until this stabilizes so all the gas has left the loop.

steps.title: Repeat steps 6-9 two times
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 6-9 two times

steps.title: Check Beam 1 Look at the...
steps.instruction: Check Beam 1 Look at the beam intensity, it should be =< ~ 1e-12. You should expect a value of around 5e-9

steps.title: V, then B
steps.instruction: V, then B : set target to 6e-9 (Enter) This is to minimize the possibility of adding technical artifacts to measurements by always analysing gas at ~ the same concentration

steps.title: Manually adjust variable volume if the...
steps.instruction: Manually adjust variable volume if the target beam intensity is below or above 6e-9 Take small steps! adjusting the volume +/-5 is sufficient in most cases. The intensity will drfit down over time as gas is consumed by the detector so always good to check both sample and reference immediately before measuring

steps.title: Run (make sure to feed new paper to the printer
steps.instruction: Run (make sure to feed new paper to the printer!)

steps.title: Open LVRun actual samples:
steps.instruction: Open LVRun actual samples:

steps.title: Go to Sample side (use F1...
steps.instruction: Go to Sample side (use F1 F1 to switch from Ref to Sample)

steps.title: Vacuum Sample side Open "SP" Open "SF" Software view
steps.instruction: Vacuum Sample side Open "SP" Open "SF" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Check Bean 1 intensity => ~...
steps.instruction: Check Bean 1 intensity => ~ 1e-12 This is to check that the lines are clean, 1e-12 is the value you get when there is little gas left in the system

steps.title: Return Variable Volume Sample to 0;...
steps.instruction: Return Variable Volume Sample to 0; V A (zero) 0 This is done to reset the bellows for injection of the next sample.

steps.title: Connect closed sample tube (SI closed)
steps.instruction: Connect closed sample tube (SI closed)

steps.title: Vacuum sample inlet Close "SF" Open...
steps.instruction: Vacuum sample inlet Close "SF" Open "SI" (SP already open) Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Pirani gauge will go to 1e-3,...
steps.instruction: Pirani gauge will go to 1e-3, wait 10 more seconds The Pirani gauge gives an indication of the gas pressure in the inlet loop. A value of 1e-3 means the loop is clean of gas so you are ready to inject.

steps.title: Isolate from pump Close "SP" Close "SI" Open "SF"
steps.instruction: Isolate from pump Close "SP" Close "SI" Open "SF"

steps.title: Carefully open collection tube (transducer value will rise) Software view
steps.instruction: Carefully open collection tube (transducer value will rise) Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrow indicates an increase in signal from the Pirani gauge as gas build up behind valve SI

steps.title: Load sample gas into system Open...
steps.instruction: Load sample gas into system Open 'SI' Wait for beam 1 intensity to stabilize. Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrow indicates direction of gas flow. Beam 1 value will rise (if not, then only water in sample; close sample immediately and vacuum!)

steps.title: Adjust reference beam intensity Switch to...
steps.instruction: Adjust reference beam intensity Switch to Ref side (F1 F1) V B 6e-9 This is done due to drift in reference beam intensity as gas is used

steps.title: Esc menu D sample ID Cold finger
steps.instruction: Esc menu D sample ID Cold finger: N Make sure there is paper in the printer!

steps.title: Note down values in lab book
steps.instruction: Note down values in lab book

steps.title: Vacuum Sample side Open "SF" Open...
steps.instruction: Vacuum Sample side Open "SF" Open "SP Return variable volume Sample to 0; V, A, 0 Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Use cursor to scroll through to...
steps.instruction: Use cursor to scroll through to bottom of page for results (don’t change anything!)

steps.title: Purge reference linesOpen "RF"Open "RP" Software view
steps.instruction: Purge reference linesOpen "RF"Open "RP" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Purge reference inletClose RF"Open "RI" Software view
steps.instruction: Purge reference inletClose RF"Open "RI" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Isolate the reference lines and prepare...
steps.instruction: Isolate the reference lines and prepare for loading reference gas Close 'RP' Open 'RF' Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Close the second valve
steps.instruction: Close the second valve

steps.title: Flood both sides of the device...
steps.instruction: Flood both sides of the device with reference gas Close "LV" vacuum line Open "RP" Open "SP"Open "SF" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Isolate valves Close "RF" Close "RI"...
steps.instruction: Isolate valves Close "RF" Close "RI" Close "SF" Close "SI" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Close off inlet Close "SI' Close "SF" Software view
steps.instruction: Close off inlet Close "SI' Close "SF" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

steps.title: Open all valves on both sides...
steps.instruction: Open all valves on both sides except SI and RI This is done to clean out the system ready for the next use. Do not turn off the vacuum pump, it should run continuosly to avoid wearing it out.
